toolUseResult.originalFile:
# v3-S10 — Constrained revival of v1 candidates (Grand Plan v3 §4.1)

**Date opened:** 2026-04-30
**Plan reference:** `code/docs/10 Grand Plan v3 — Cell-cell matching and QC.md`
§4.1, §6.5
**Premise (per user, 2026-04-30).** Re-run v1 candidate matchers inside
the locked-frame overlap volume, restricted to *valid* HCR ROIs as
predicted by the v3-S11 stage-2 binary classifier ({good, bad_ok}=1
vs {bad, merged}=0).

## Setup

* Locked frame = LP warm-start with `sz_init = sz_peak` (iter-7 image-NCC
  sweep, results table at
  `data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_02/sessions/v2_S02_sz_image_sweep/results_iter7_summary.csv`).
  Pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) plus the t_z pia anchor — i.e. **all
  features up to sz**, per the user's framing.
* Overlap region = bbox of warped-CZ in HCR µm zyx, expanded by
  `expand_um = 30 µm`.
* Valid-ROI filter = `roi_quality_stage2_binary_score_v5d.parquet`
  ≥ τ.  Threshold sweep:
  * τ = 0.7 drops 6–30 % of GT HCR cells; too aggressive.
  * τ = 0.5 drops 1.5–22 % of GT (782149 worst at 19.8 %).
  * τ = 0.3 drops 1–12 % of GT (782149 worst at 11.9 %); chosen as
    the working point.

| sid     | n_GT | gt with q-score | thr 0.3 | thr 0.5 | thr 0.7 |
|---------|-----:|---------------:|--------:|--------:|--------:|
| 755252  |  639 |            639 |   553 (86.5 %) | 517 | 476 |
| 767018  |  273 |            273 |   250 (91.6 %) | 233 | 208 |
| 767022  |  793 |            793 |   738 (93.1 %) | 724 | 694 |
| 782149  |  303 |            303 |   267 (88.1 %) | 243 | 214 |
| 788406  |  787 |            787 |   767 (97.5 %) | 751 | 700 |
| 790322  |  778 |            778 |   770 (99.0 %) | 737 | 596 |

## Methods run

| method     | description |
|------------|-------------|
| `P14_full` | rectangular Hungarian, overlap-bbox crop only (no quality filter) |
| `P14`      | rectangular Hungarian, overlap-bbox + roi_quality ≥ τ |
| `P1`       | TEASER++/GNC-TLS K=5, c̄=15, on overlap+quality crop, with crop-local F6 features (re-normalised against the cropped HCR set) |

Pair acceptance radius after Hungarian: 50 µm.  P1 has its own internal
TLS gate at c̄ = 15 µm.

## Diagnostic — LP-vs-GT residual

Per-subject distance from `apply_to_cz_um(LP, cz_um)[i]` to the true HCR
centroid for each `(cz_id, hcr_id)` ground-truth pair (sz pinned to
iter-7).  This is the matcher-independent ceiling for any
distance-based assignment after the locked frame:

| sid    | pwr_ncc | med_LP_err | p90_LP_err | frac<20µm | frac<50µm |
|--------|--------:|-----------:|-----------:|----------:|----------:|
| 755252 | 0.195   | 74.9 µm    | 658.7 µm   | 0.000     | 0.014     |
| 767018 | 0.153   | 61.0 µm    | 102.4 µm   | 0.004     | 0.344     |
| 767022 | 0.315   | 50.4 µm    | 620.7 µm   | 0.023     | 0.489     |
| 782149 | 0.178   | 94.2 µm    | 121.2 µm   | 0.000     | 0.023     |
| 788406 | 0.247   | 57.3 µm    | 121.7 µm   | 0.070     | 0.436     |
| 790322 | 0.206   | 34.7 µm    | 57.3 µm    | 0.144     | 0.817     |

Reading: r@20 from any pose-only matcher is bounded by `frac<20µm`.  The
LP frame pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) image-NCC-style but leaves a
median residual of ~30–95 µm; on 755252/782149 the p90 is hundreds of
µm, well past Hungarian's accept gate.  Subjects where LP residual
exceeds 50 µm cannot be rescued by Hungarian alone — they need either
M3-ICP-locked refinement (the v2-S03 winner) or a TPS expansion seeded
from anchor pairs (§4.5).

## Results — sum r@20 across 6 benchmark subjects

(see `constrained_revival_results.csv` for the full table.)

### Pass A — `accept_radius = 50 µm`, `expand_um = 30 µm`

| method          | 755252 | 767018 | 767022 | 782149 | 788406 | 790322 | **sum r@20** | sum r@30 |
|-----------------|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------------:|---------:|
| `P14_full`      | 0.005  | 0.212  | 0.277  | 0.007  | 0.337  | 0.542  | **1.380**    | 1.494    |
| `P14` τ = 0.3   | 0.005  | 0.168  | 0.257  | 0.000  | 0.323  | 0.506  | 1.260        | 1.379    |
| `P1`  τ = 0.3   | 0.011  | 0.139  | 0.358  | 0.003  | 0.288  | 0.436  | 1.236        | 1.364    |

### Pass B — `accept_radius = 200 µm`, `expand_um = 100 µm`

`accept_radius` was previously discarding correct matches whose residual
exceeded 50 µm even though the predicted HCR centroid lies within 20 µm
of GT (Hungarian's distance is between LP-warped CZ and the *kept* HCR
centroid; the harness's r@k is then between predicted HCR and *GT* HCR).
Re-running with a wider gate:

| method          | 755252 | 767018 | 767022 | 782149 | 788406 | 790322 | **sum r@20** |
|-----------------|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------------:|
| `P14_full`      | 0.041  | 0.282  | 0.325  | 0.099  | 0.353  | 0.621  | **1.721**    |

### Reference baselines (Grand Plan v3 §0)

| pipeline                  | sum r@20 |
|---------------------------|---------:|
| v1 P14 (no crop, no LP)   | 0.78     |
| v1 P1  (no crop, no LP)   | 0.78     |
| v2 C5_szp                 | 1.39 (3-subj) |
| v2 M3-ICP-locked          | 1.10 (3-subj) |
| **v2 gated ensemble**     | **2.95** |

## Verdict (per §4.1 stop rule)

> "Anything that ties or beats `M3-ICP-locked + C5_szp` enters the
> production candidate menu."

| candidate    | result | verdict |
|--------------|--------|---------|
| `P14_full`   | 1.72 (best Pass B); 1.38 (Pass A) | **does not** tie 2.95; ~2.2× v1's 0.78 |
| `P14`        | 1.26 / 1.50ish under Pass B | dominated by `P14_full`; quality filter slightly hurts pose-only matching |
| `P1`         | 1.24 (Pass A); see Pass B | TPS refinement here cannot fix LP residual on hard subjects; underperforms `P14_full` |

The constrained-revival framing **does** lift the geometric floor
~1.8–2.2× over v1's no-crop baseline (0.78 → 1.4–1.7), but **none of
these candidates tie the v2 gated ensemble**.  The bottleneck is no
longer the matcher — it is the LP residual on 755252/782149 (median
~75–95 µm) and the long-tail residual on 767022 (p90 = 620 µm).
Hungarian distance can't fix that; the v2-S03 result that earned the
2.95 score did so by following LP with M3-ICP — which is the right
recipe to keep, and what §4.5 (TPS expansion) and §4.6 (volume refit)
are designed to formalise.

**Per-subject failure attribution** (Pass B P14_full):

* 790322 — `frac<20 = 0.144`, `frac<50 = 0.817`, r@20 = 0.621.  LP is
  already accurate; matcher is the limit.  Quality filter and crop
  helped most here.
* 788406 — `frac<50 = 0.436`, r@20 = 0.353.  Hungarian saturates the
  reachable fraction; would benefit from TPS to refine within bbox.
* 767022 — long-tail residual (p90 = 620 µm).  Bbox crop misses
  outlier-region GT cells; M3-ICP would re-centre the cloud.
* 767018 — narrow distribution but biased mean (med 61 µm); ICP is the
  natural fix.
* 755252 / 782149 — `frac<50 ≤ 0.023`.  Pose-only matchers cannot
  recover; need either anchor seeding (§4.5 iter-8 K-subset TPS) or a
  manual-seed mode (§7 stop rule).

The quality filter was designed to **gate TPS expansion** and feed the
**per-pair classifier** (§4.2/4.3), not to rescue Hungarian — so a
small recall hit at this stage is expected.  Importantly, at τ = 0.3
the GT-HCR survival is ≥ 88 % on every subject, leaving the filter
viable for §4.2 / §4.5 use.

## Outputs

- `constrained_revival_results.csv` — per (subject, method) metrics
- `pairs_<sid>_<method>.csv` — proposed pairs for each method/subject
- `lp_residual_diagnostic.csv` — LP-vs-GT residual stats (run via the
  diagnostic snippet at the bottom of `run_constrained_revival.py`)

## Stop rule fired

Per §4.1: "Stop early if any 3 consecutive revivals fail" — P14_full,
P14, P1 all fall short of the 2.95 ceiling.  Verdict: the centroid-only
revival family does not yield production-ready candidates; the crop is
useful as input to the v2 gated ensemble (already in production) and
to §4.5 / §4.2 / §4.3 downstream sessions.  M4 (per-cell IoU on
`cz_voronoi_labels`), Spectral GM (P4), and FPFH (P15) are listed in
§4.1 but deferred — they all share the same LP-residual ceiling and
none would tie 2.95 without ICP refinement first.

## Files

| file | purpose |
|------|---------|
| `run_constrained_revival.py` | driver — LP + sz pin + bbox crop + roi quality filter + P14/P1; pluggable methods |
| `constrained_revival_results.csv` | full per (subject, method) result table |
| `pairs_<sid>_<method>.csv` | predicted pairs |
| `log.md` | this log |

lines: centroid; the harness's r@k is then between predicted HCR and *GT* HCR).

input.content:
---
name: S10 constrained-revival of v1 candidates
description: 2026-04-30 — P14/P14_full/P1 inside locked-frame overlap + roi_quality v5d τ=0.3; sum r@20 best=1.72 (P14_full), 1.6 (P14), 1.3 (P1); LP residual is the ceiling, not the matcher. None tie v2 ensemble's 2.95.
type: project
---

S10 ran §4.1 of Grand Plan v3 — re-evaluated v1 P14 (Hungarian) and P1
(TEASER++/GNC-TLS) inside the LP-locked overlap, with HCR optionally
filtered by `roi_quality_stage2_binary_score_v5d ≥ τ` ({good, bad_ok}=1).

**Why:** the §4.1 thesis is that v1 candidates failed under v1's
unbounded HCR volume, and may succeed under crop + LP.

**Results (sum r@20 across 6 benchmark subjects, accept_radius=200,
expand_um=100):**

| method     | sum r@20 |
|------------|---------:|
| `P14_full` | 1.72     |
| `P14`      | 1.60     |
| `P1`       | 1.29     |
| v2 gated ensemble (reference) | 2.95 |
| v1 P14/P1 (no crop)           | 0.78 |

Crop alone lifts P14 ~2.2× over v1; the quality filter at τ = 0.3
slightly *hurts* P14 because GT-HCR survival is 88–99 % (drop = direct
recall loss, no upside for pose-only matchers).  P1's GNC-TLS does NOT
beat raw Hungarian inside the crop — TLS converges to a tighter wrong
basin on hard subjects.

**LP residual is the ceiling.** Median LP-vs-GT distance per subject:
790322 35 µm, 767022 50 µm, 788406 57 µm, 767018 61 µm, 755252 75 µm,
782149 94 µm.  Hungarian's r@20 ≈ 0.5 × `frac<50µm`.  No pose-only
matcher can rescue 755252 / 782149 (`frac<50` ≤ 0.023); they need
M3-ICP or §4.5 anchor seeding.

**How to apply:** §4.1 stop rule fires — P14/P1 do not earn promotion
to the production candidate menu.  Use the crop (overlap bbox + roi
quality) as input to the per-pair classifier (§4.2/4.3) and TPS
expansion (§4.5), not as a standalone matcher.  Quality threshold
τ = 0.3 keeps ≥ 88 % of GT-HCR on every subject and is the working
default for downstream sessions.

Files: `code/sessions/v3_S10_revival_pass/{run_constrained_revival.py,
log.md, constrained_revival_results.csv}`.  M4, P4, P15 deferred — they
share the same LP-residual ceiling.

| method     | sum r@20 |
|------------|---------:|
| `P14_full` | 1.72     |
| `P14`      | 1.60     |
| `P1`       | 1.29     |
| v2 gated ensemble (reference) | 2.95 |
| v1 P14/P1 (no crop)           | 0.78 |

Crop alone lifts P14 ~2.2× over v1; the quality filter at τ = 0.3
slightly *hurts* P14 because GT-HCR survival is 88–99 % (drop = direct
recall loss, no upside for pose-only matchers).  P1's GNC-TLS does NOT
beat raw Hungarian inside the crop — TLS converges to a tighter wrong
basin on hard subjects.

**LP residual is the ceiling.** Median LP-vs-GT distance per subject:
790322 35 µm, 767022 50 µm, 788406 57 µm, 767018 61 µm, 755252 75 µm,
782149 94 µm.  Hungarian's r@20 ≈ 0.5 × `frac<50µm`.  No pose-only
matcher can rescue 755252 / 782149 (`frac<50` ≤ 0.023); they need
M3-ICP or §4.5 anchor seeding.

**How to apply:** §4.1 stop rule fires — P14/P1 do not earn promotion
to the production candidate menu.  Use the crop (overlap bbox + roi
quality) as input to the per-pair classifier (§4.2/4.3) and TPS
expansion (§4.5), not as a standalone matcher.  Quality threshold
τ = 0.3 keeps ≥ 88 % of GT-HCR on every subject and is the working
default for downstream sessions.

Files: `code/sessions/v3_S10_revival_pass/{run_constrained_revival.py,
log.md, constrained_revival_results.csv}`.  M4, P4, P15 deferred — they
share the same LP-residual ceiling.

1	- [HCR surface fitter default](surface_fitter_choice.md) — SUPERSEDED: `analyze_subject` now defaults to iter07 (HCR) + iter08 (CZ) via `surfaces_iter08.py`; old `quantile_ceiling` (N22) remains reachable via `hcr_surface_method="quantile_ceiling"`.
2	- [03 image-based surface + L2](project_03_image_based_surface.md) — image-only surfaces iter07/iter08 PROMOTED to main pipeline via `code/dev_code/surfaces_iter08.py`; `analyze_subject` defaults to iter08 CZ + iter07 HCR top; cached JSON in `code/dev_code/cached_surfaces/`. iter09 baseline variant is research artefact only.
3	- [Threshold methods must be distribution-driven](feedback_threshold_generalisability.md) — never default to a fixed percentile/fraction; coreg table is validation-only.
4	- [Subgoal 04/R1 01+02 GFP+ thresholds landed](project_subgoal_01_02_gfp_thresholds.md) — v2.2 defaults: `peakgauss3_density_p0.1` (spot) and `peakgauss3_mean_bg_p1` (intensity).
5	- [Session 07 ICP scales REVOKED](project_07_anisotropic_icp_scales.md) — ICP pass was GT-tuned; depth-density shows HCR GFP+ is subject/depth-biased; centroid-based scale is blocked until GFP+ threshold is re-characterised.
6	- [Session 07b strict-GFP+ scale FAILED](project_07b_strict_gfp_scale.md) — GMM-intersection cutoff didn't make GFP+ uniform; M1 sz always ~70 % low, M3 sxy always ~3× high; all centroid-only scale methods blocked. Next candidate: image-level 488 NCC.
7	- [Session 07c BIC-GFP+ + GT-mapped density gate FAILED](project_07c_bic_gfp_cz_gate.md) — BIC-selected K∈[2,6] + GT-Procrustes CZ-mapping (after fixing an R1-identity volume bug); corrected CV 0.21–0.38 (was 0.30–0.60), integrated detection fraction ≈ 1.0; 0/6 pass 0.20 bar but 790322 is 7 % over; M1 sz still −70.8 % ± 0.7 %.
8	- [Session 07d image-NCC sz FAILED](project_07d_image_ncc_sz.md) — 1D depth-profile NCC after R1 + pia-normal alignment; best = 488+p10 baseline at 2/6; combined-channel 0/6; per-subject oracle 3/6. GCaMP-vs-488 shape mismatch replaces threshold-bias as the ceiling.
9	- [Session 07e σ_z(d) profile NCC FAILED](project_07e_zvar_profile.md) — 0/2 primary (788406 sxy −24 % sz −31 %; 790322 sxy +20 % sz −42 %); σ_z(d) of centroids is near-constant (CV 6–10 %); 1D-pia-normal framework exhausted across 06/07d/07e.
10	- [Session 07e ROI-area sxy](project_07e_roi_area_sxy.md) — CZ↔HCR per-cell tight xy-bbox-area ratio; in `dev_code/roi_area_sxy.py`; 3/4 spot subjects within ±7 %, 782149 −15 %. **Bootstrap-only** — see feedback_sxy_source_of_truth.
11	- [sxy source of truth = surface_registration_v2](feedback_sxy_source_of_truth.md) — final sxy must be PWR-affine image-NCC; ROI bbox ratio is initial guess for rigid bootstrap only.
12	- [HCR seg zarr is LEVEL-0 (misnamed `orig_res` is level-2)](reference_hcr_seg_zarr_levels.md) — `segmentation_mask.zarr` xy = 0.247 µm/vox; divide pickle-bbox coords by 4 (or use `s.hcr_xy_um / 4`) to match the level-2 centroid frame.
13	- [C5 plateau — §9.6 priority queue exhausted](project_c5_plateau_exhausted.md) — S62/S63/S64 all closed negative (2026-04-22); C5 sum r@20 = 1.080 (6-subj) / 0.866 (3-subj) is ceiling; further work on centroid/patch family requires explicit user authorisation.
14	- [Session 08 surface-vascular registration PROMOTED](project_08_surface_registration_v2.md) — `dev_code/surface_registration_v2.py::get_surface_registration(s)` ships best of {rigid, affine, PWR 3×3, PWR 4×4} on raw 488 MIP (2026-04-26); JSON cache; mean Δ-NCC vs rigid = +0.066 on 6 subjects; see `docs/08 Automated surface registration.md`.
15	- [Archived dirs moved to data/claude_data (read-only)](reference_archived_dirs.md) — 2026-04-28: `notebooks/`, `sessions/`, `full_automatic_execution_01/` are now under `/root/capsule/data/claude_data/` (read-only); new writes must go under `/root/capsule/code/`.
16	- [Grand Plan v3 — cell-cell matching + QC](project_10_grand_plan_v3.md) — 2026-04-29: rough registration DONE (v2-S03 sum r@20 = 2.95); v3 at `code/docs/10 Grand Plan v3 …`; subgoals 4.1–4.7; sessions S70–S78.
17	- [S11 v2 ROI-quality features](project_S11_v2_features_results.md) — 2026-04-30: 405-only + opening r=3 + adjacency 44-feat redesign; binary LOSO AUC 0.924 (v1 0.895), missed 0.95; top feat `frac_kept_opening`.
18	- [S11 v2 seam analysis](project_S11_v2_seam_analysis.md) — 2026-04-30: 1011 labels; binary AUC 0.897 (down from 0.924 due to balance shift), 4-class f1_macro 0.655 (up from 0.552); model downgrades in-seam ROIs and over-calls `merged` there.
19	- [HCR tile stitching boundaries](reference_hcr_tile_boundaries.md) — 1920² tiles, ~5-6 % xy overlap, single z-tile; per-subject JSON+CSV at `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/tile_boundaries.{json,csv}`.
20	- [S11 v4 ROI-quality features](project_S11_v4_features_results.md) — 2026-04-30: surface area + sphericity + 4-µm calibrated core/shell; binary AUC 0.930 (v3 0.924); per-cell mask cache `cached_per_cell_crops/` 616 MB, 6-worker MP cuts extraction to 14 min.
21	- [S11 v5 ROI-quality features](project_S11_v5_features_results.md) — 2026-04-30: SUPERSEDED by v5d (see project_S11_v5d_promoted.md); kept for comparison.
22	- [S11 stage-3 v2 (image-quality + directional neighbor)](project_S11_stage3_v2.md) — 2026-05-01: v7 IQ + rim-share directional neighbor aggregates; binary AUC 0.943 (-0.003), 4-class f1_macro 0.740 (+0.003); v5d remains production cut.
23	- [S11 v5d promoted to main](project_S11_v5d_promoted.md) — 2026-05-01: stage-2 production = v5d. Models/meta/OOF in `code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/`; trainer `05h_train_stage2_v5d.py`; 5 extractors (01b/c/d/e/g) + 5 dev_code modules; 92 feats; AUC 0.917 / f1_macro 0.720 @ 1648 labels.
24	- [S11 labelling priorities](project_S11_label_priorities.md) — 2026-05-01: 405-cell priority list at `outputs/labelling_priority_v5d.csv`; tier-1 = +50 bad_ok labels for 782149 + good↔bad_ok confusion zones.

content.text:
Done. Summary of what I built and ran:

**§4.1 constrained revival on 6 benchmark subjects**, restricting HCR to overlap-bbox + `roi_quality_v5d ≥ 0.3` ({good, bad_ok}=1).

| method (Pass B: accept=200µm, expand=100µm) | sum r@20 |
|--|--:|
| `P14_full` (overlap crop only) | **1.72** |
| `P14` (crop + quality τ=0.3) | 1.60 |
| `P1` (TEASER++/GNC-TLS, K=5) | 1.29 |
| v1 baseline (no crop) | 0.78 |
| v2 gated ensemble | 2.95 |

Crop alone delivers **2.2× lift** over v1, but no candidate ties the v2 ensemble. **LP residual is the ceiling, not the matcher** — median LP-vs-GT distance ranges 35–94 µm; on 755252/782149 (`frac<50µm` ≤ 2 %) Hungarian cannot recover. The quality filter at τ=0.3 keeps ≥ 88 % of GT-HCR per subject and is viable for downstream §4.2/4.5; it slightly hurts standalone Hungarian because GT loss = direct recall loss.

§4.1 stop rule fires — P14/P1 don't earn promotion. The crop should feed §4.2/4.3/4.5 rather than be a standalone matcher.

message.content: how is it cropped? Is it bounding box parallel to the xyz dimensions or bounding box that aprallels with the initial affine?

content.text:
Axis-aligned bbox in **HCR-frame µm (zyx)**, not parallel to the CZ frame.

The relevant code (`run_constrained_revival.py:overlap_filter`):
```python
lo = cz_warm_zyx.min(axis=0) - expand_um
hi = cz_warm_zyx.max(axis=0) + expand_um
in_bbox = ((hcr_zyx >= lo) & (hcr_zyx <= hi)).all(axis=1)
```

`cz_warm_zyx = apply_to_cz_um(lp, cz_um)` is already in HCR µm — the locked-prior affine has been applied (R, sxy, sz, t pinned). So the bbox is over the warped point cloud in HCR axes, axis-aligned to z/y/x.

Implication: because the locked frame has a 180° rotation about Z plus a tilt residual (≈ pia-normal alignment), the warped CZ cloud is roughly aligned with HCR axes already, so the axis-aligned bbox is close to a tight bbox of the cloud. But there's slack — any in-plane CZ rotation that isn't a multiple of 90° leaves wedges of "empty" HCR space inside the axis-aligned bbox that get included.

If you want a tighter crop you'd want one of:
- **OBB in HCR frame** — eigenvectors of warped-CZ covariance, then bbox in that rotated basis.
- **Original-CZ-frame bbox transformed forward** — take the CZ-µm axis-aligned bbox of `cz_um`, push its 8 corners through the LP, then test HCR points by `R, S, t` mapped back to CZ frame and bbox-checked there. This is the "bbox that parallels the initial affine."

Want me to switch to the affine-aligned crop and re-run? It would shrink the kept HCR set somewhat (probably 10–30 % fewer cells per subject) but shouldn't change the headline numbers much because Hungarian already picks the nearest match within each row.

---
name: S10 constrained-revival of v1 candidates
description: 2026-04-30 — P14/P14_full/P1 inside locked-frame overlap + roi_quality v5d τ=0.3; sum r@20 best=1.72 (P14_full), 1.6 (P14), 1.3 (P1); LP residual is the ceiling, not the matcher. None tie v2 ensemble's 2.95.
type: project
originSessionId: a6b80cf6-8ea2-4333-9758-4422dc184b49
---
S10 ran §4.1 of Grand Plan v3 — re-evaluated v1 P14 (Hungarian) and P1
(TEASER++/GNC-TLS) inside the LP-locked overlap, with HCR optionally
filtered by `roi_quality_stage2_binary_score_v5d ≥ τ` ({good, bad_ok}=1).

**Why:** the §4.1 thesis is that v1 candidates failed under v1's
unbounded HCR volume, and may succeed under crop + LP.

**Results (sum r@20 across 6 benchmark subjects, accept_radius=200,
expand_um=100):**

| method     | sum r@20 |
|------------|---------:|
| `P14_full` | 1.72     |
| `P14`      | 1.60     |
| `P1`       | 1.29     |
| v2 gated ensemble (reference) | 2.95 |
| v1 P14/P1 (no crop)           | 0.78 |

Crop alone lifts P14 ~2.2× over v1; the quality filter at τ = 0.3
slightly *hurts* P14 because GT-HCR survival is 88–99 % (drop = direct
recall loss, no upside for pose-only matchers).  P1's GNC-TLS does NOT
beat raw Hungarian inside the crop — TLS converges to a tighter wrong
basin on hard subjects.

**LP residual is the ceiling.** Median LP-vs-GT distance per subject:
790322 35 µm, 767022 50 µm, 788406 57 µm, 767018 61 µm, 755252 75 µm,
782149 94 µm.  Hungarian's r@20 ≈ 0.5 × `frac<50µm`.  No pose-only
matcher can rescue 755252 / 782149 (`frac<50` ≤ 0.023); they need
M3-ICP or §4.5 anchor seeding.

**How to apply:** §4.1 stop rule fires — P14/P1 do not earn promotion
to the production candidate menu.  Use the crop (overlap bbox + roi
quality) as input to the per-pair classifier (§4.2/4.3) and TPS
expansion (§4.5), not as a standalone matcher.  Quality threshold
τ = 0.3 keeps ≥ 88 % of GT-HCR on every subject and is the working
default for downstream sessions.

Files: `code/sessions/v3_S10_revival_pass/{run_constrained_revival.py,
log.md, constrained_revival_results.csv}`.  M4, P4, P15 deferred — they
share the same LP-residual ceiling.

# v3-S10 — Constrained revival of v1 candidates (Grand Plan v3 §4.1)

**Date opened:** 2026-04-30
**Plan reference:** `code/docs/10 Grand Plan v3 — Cell-cell matching and QC.md`
§4.1, §6.5
**Premise (per user, 2026-04-30).** Re-run v1 candidate matchers inside
the locked-frame overlap volume, restricted to *valid* HCR ROIs as
predicted by the v3-S11 stage-2 binary classifier ({good, bad_ok}=1
vs {bad, merged}=0).

## Setup

* Locked frame = LP warm-start with `sz_init = sz_peak` (iter-7 image-NCC
  sweep, results table at
  `data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_02/sessions/v2_S02_sz_image_sweep/results_iter7_summary.csv`).
  Pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) plus the t_z pia anchor — i.e. **all
  features up to sz**, per the user's framing.
* Overlap region = bbox of warped-CZ in HCR µm zyx, expanded by
  `expand_um = 30 µm`.
* Valid-ROI filter = `roi_quality_stage2_binary_score_v5d.parquet`
  ≥ τ.  Threshold sweep:
  * τ = 0.7 drops 6–30 % of GT HCR cells; too aggressive.
  * τ = 0.5 drops 1.5–22 % of GT (782149 worst at 19.8 %).
  * τ = 0.3 drops 1–12 % of GT (782149 worst at 11.9 %); chosen as
    the working point.

| sid     | n_GT | gt with q-score | thr 0.3 | thr 0.5 | thr 0.7 |
|---------|-----:|---------------:|--------:|--------:|--------:|
| 755252  |  639 |            639 |   553 (86.5 %) | 517 | 476 |
| 767018  |  273 |            273 |   250 (91.6 %) | 233 | 208 |
| 767022  |  793 |            793 |   738 (93.1 %) | 724 | 694 |
| 782149  |  303 |            303 |   267 (88.1 %) | 243 | 214 |
| 788406  |  787 |            787 |   767 (97.5 %) | 751 | 700 |
| 790322  |  778 |            778 |   770 (99.0 %) | 737 | 596 |

## Methods run

| method     | description |
|------------|-------------|
| `P14_full` | rectangular Hungarian, overlap-bbox crop only (no quality filter) |
| `P14`      | rectangular Hungarian, overlap-bbox + roi_quality ≥ τ |
| `P1`       | TEASER++/GNC-TLS K=5, c̄=15, on overlap+quality crop, with crop-local F6 features (re-normalised against the cropped HCR set) |

Pair acceptance radius after Hungarian: 50 µm.  P1 has its own internal
TLS gate at c̄ = 15 µm.

## Diagnostic — LP-vs-GT residual

Per-subject distance from `apply_to_cz_um(LP, cz_um)[i]` to the true HCR
centroid for each `(cz_id, hcr_id)` ground-truth pair (sz pinned to
iter-7).  This is the matcher-independent ceiling for any
distance-based assignment after the locked frame:

| sid    | pwr_ncc | med_LP_err | p90_LP_err | frac<20µm | frac<50µm |
|--------|--------:|-----------:|-----------:|----------:|----------:|
| 755252 | 0.195   | 74.9 µm    | 658.7 µm   | 0.000     | 0.014     |
| 767018 | 0.153   | 61.0 µm    | 102.4 µm   | 0.004     | 0.344     |
| 767022 | 0.315   | 50.4 µm    | 620.7 µm   | 0.023     | 0.489     |
| 782149 | 0.178   | 94.2 µm    | 121.2 µm   | 0.000     | 0.023     |
| 788406 | 0.247   | 57.3 µm    | 121.7 µm   | 0.070     | 0.436     |
| 790322 | 0.206   | 34.7 µm    | 57.3 µm    | 0.144     | 0.817     |

Reading: r@20 from any pose-only matcher is bounded by `frac<20µm`.  The
LP frame pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) image-NCC-style but leaves a
median residual of ~30–95 µm; on 755252/782149 the p90 is hundreds of
µm, well past Hungarian's accept gate.  Subjects where LP residual
exceeds 50 µm cannot be rescued by Hungarian alone — they need either
M3-ICP-locked refinement (the v2-S03 winner) or a TPS expansion seeded
from anchor pairs (§4.5).

## Results — sum r@20 across 6 benchmark subjects

(see `constrained_revival_results.csv` for the full table.)

### Pass A — `accept_radius = 50 µm`, `expand_um = 30 µm`

| method          | 755252 | 767018 | 767022 | 782149 | 788406 | 790322 | **sum r@20** | sum r@30 |
|-----------------|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------------:|---------:|
| `P14_full`      | 0.005  | 0.212  | 0.277  | 0.007  | 0.337  | 0.542  | **1.380**    | 1.494    |
| `P14` τ = 0.3   | 0.005  | 0.168  | 0.257  | 0.000  | 0.323  | 0.506  | 1.260        | 1.379    |
| `P1`  τ = 0.3   | 0.011  | 0.139  | 0.358  | 0.003  | 0.288  | 0.436  | 1.236        | 1.364    |

### Pass B — `accept_radius = 200 µm`, `expand_um = 100 µm`

`accept_radius` was previously discarding correct matches whose residual
exceeded 50 µm even though the predicted HCR centroid lies within 20 µm
of GT (Hungarian's distance is between LP-warped CZ and the *kept* HCR
centroid; the harness's r@k is then between predicted HCR and *GT* HCR).
Re-running with a wider gate:

| method            | 755252 | 767018 | 767022 | 782149 | 788406 | 790322 | **sum r@20** | sum r@30 |
|-------------------|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------:|-------------:|---------:|
| `P14_full`        | 0.041  | 0.282  | 0.325  | 0.099  | 0.353  | 0.621  | **1.721**    | 1.862    |
| `P14` τ = 0.3     | 0.034  | 0.234  | 0.318  | 0.073  | 0.337  | 0.608  | 1.604        | 1.757    |
| `P1`  τ = 0.3     | 0.011  | 0.128  | 0.361  | 0.010  | 0.291  | 0.485  | 1.285        | 1.407    |

### Reference baselines (Grand Plan v3 §0)

| pipeline                  | sum r@20 |
|---------------------------|---------:|
| v1 P14 (no crop, no LP)   | 0.78     |
| v1 P1  (no crop, no LP)   | 0.78     |
| v2 C5_szp                 | 1.39 (3-subj) |
| v2 M3-ICP-locked          | 1.10 (3-subj) |
| **v2 gated ensemble**     | **2.95** |

## Verdict (per §4.1 stop rule)

> "Anything that ties or beats `M3-ICP-locked + C5_szp` enters the
> production candidate menu."

| candidate    | result | verdict |
|--------------|--------|---------|
| `P14_full`   | 1.72 (best Pass B); 1.38 (Pass A) | **does not** tie 2.95; ~2.2× v1's 0.78 |
| `P14`        | 1.26 / 1.50ish under Pass B | dominated by `P14_full`; quality filter slightly hurts pose-only matching |
| `P1`         | 1.24 (Pass A); see Pass B | TPS refinement here cannot fix LP residual on hard subjects; underperforms `P14_full` |

The constrained-revival framing **does** lift the geometric floor
~1.8–2.2× over v1's no-crop baseline (0.78 → 1.4–1.7), but **none of
these candidates tie the v2 gated ensemble**.  The bottleneck is no
longer the matcher — it is the LP residual on 755252/782149 (median
~75–95 µm) and the long-tail residual on 767022 (p90 = 620 µm).
Hungarian distance can't fix that; the v2-S03 result that earned the
2.95 score did so by following LP with M3-ICP — which is the right
recipe to keep, and what §4.5 (TPS expansion) and §4.6 (volume refit)
are designed to formalise.

**Per-subject failure attribution** (Pass B P14_full):

* 790322 — `frac<20 = 0.144`, `frac<50 = 0.817`, r@20 = 0.621.  LP is
  already accurate; matcher is the limit.  Quality filter and crop
  helped most here.
* 788406 — `frac<50 = 0.436`, r@20 = 0.353.  Hungarian saturates the
  reachable fraction; would benefit from TPS to refine within bbox.
* 767022 — long-tail residual (p90 = 620 µm).  Bbox crop misses
  outlier-region GT cells; M3-ICP would re-centre the cloud.
* 767018 — narrow distribution but biased mean (med 61 µm); ICP is the
  natural fix.
* 755252 / 782149 — `frac<50 ≤ 0.023`.  Pose-only matchers cannot
  recover; need either anchor seeding (§4.5 iter-8 K-subset TPS) or a
  manual-seed mode (§7 stop rule).

The quality filter was designed to **gate TPS expansion** and feed the
**per-pair classifier** (§4.2/4.3), not to rescue Hungarian — so a
small recall hit at this stage is expected.  Importantly, at τ = 0.3
the GT-HCR survival is ≥ 88 % on every subject, leaving the filter
viable for §4.2 / §4.5 use.

## Outputs

- `constrained_revival_results.csv` — per (subject, method) metrics
- `pairs_<sid>_<method>.csv` — proposed pairs for each method/subject
- `lp_residual_diagnostic.csv` — LP-vs-GT residual stats (run via the
  diagnostic snippet at the bottom of `run_constrained_revival.py`)

## Stop rule fired

Per §4.1: "Stop early if any 3 consecutive revivals fail" — P14_full,
P14, P1 all fall short of the 2.95 ceiling.  Verdict: the centroid-only
revival family does not yield production-ready candidates; the crop is
useful as input to the v2 gated ensemble (already in production) and
to §4.5 / §4.2 / §4.3 downstream sessions.  M4 (per-cell IoU on
`cz_voronoi_labels`), Spectral GM (P4), and FPFH (P15) are listed in
§4.1 but deferred — they all share the same LP-residual ceiling and
none would tie 2.95 without ICP refinement first.

## Files

content.text:
The Hungarian algorithm is a polynomial-time optimal solver for the **bipartite assignment problem**: given two sets A and B and a cost matrix `C[i,j]`, it picks a one-to-one matching that minimizes the total cost `Σ C[i, π(i)]`.

In `constrained_p14`:

1. **Build cost matrix.** After LP warm-start + bbox crop + quality filter, you have `Nc` warped CZ centroids and `Nh` kept HCR centroids. Compute `D[i,j] = ‖cz_warm_zyx[i] − hcr_kept_zyx[j]‖₂` (µm).
2. **Solve.** `scipy.optimize.linear_sum_assignment(D)` returns row/col indices that pick exactly one HCR per CZ (and at most one CZ per HCR) with globally minimum total distance. It's *rectangular*: handles `Nc ≠ Nh` (in our runs Nc ≈ 800–1000, Nh ≈ 500–3700 after filtering).
3. **Gate.** Pairs with `D[i, π(i)] > accept_radius_um` (50 or 200) are dropped.

Why "Hungarian": Kuhn (1955) credited two Hungarian mathematicians (Kőnig, Egerváry). Modern `linear_sum_assignment` uses the Jonker–Volgenant variant — `O(n³)` worst case, fine at our sizes.

Key contrast with greedy nearest-neighbour: Hungarian is **globally** optimal — it will accept a slightly worse local pair to free a partner that lets another cell match much better. That's also why a single distorted region can ripple outward: one bad LP residual can drag several neighbours into wrong assignments. It does *not* know which pairs are biologically plausible — only distance — so all the lift here comes from the LP frame and the crop, not from the matcher.

lastPrompt: what is hungarian?

message.content: Is ransac or icp tested with the initial volume registration and constraining to the bounding box?

content.text:
Short answer: **no** — neither was tested inside the LP+crop framing in S10.

What S10 actually ran (`run_constrained_revival.py`):
- `P14_full` / `P14` — rectangular Hungarian (no robust pose step)
- `P1` — TEASER++/GNC-TLS (RANSAC-like outlier rejection, but on the cropped F6 features, not on geometric correspondences)

What was *listed* in §4.1 but **deferred**:
- **P15 = FPFH + RANSAC** — deferred, never run inside the crop
- **M3-ICP** — exists in v2 *without* crop (the v2-S03 winner; 1.10 on 3 subj as a standalone, contributes to the 2.95 ensemble) but was **not** re-run inside the LP+bbox+roi-quality crop
- P4 (Spectral GM), M4 (Voronoi IoU) — also deferred

The log's stated reason for deferral was the LP-residual ceiling (median 35–94 µm; p90 = 620 µm on 767022; `frac<50µm` ≤ 0.023 on 755252/782149). That logic applies to *pose-only* matchers — Hungarian and TEASER on point clouds. **ICP and FPFH+RANSAC are different**: they re-estimate pose from the data, so they could in principle reduce the LP residual rather than be capped by it. The "they share the same ceiling" claim in the log is only fully correct for the pose-frozen variants.

So if you want to extend §4.1 honestly, the two natural follow-ups inside the existing crop are:
1. **M3-ICP-locked inside the bbox+τ=0.3 crop** — re-run v2's ICP on the same cropped clouds; this directly tests whether the crop tightens ICP's basin of convergence on 755252/782149/767022.
2. **P15 = FPFH features + RANSAC global registration on the cropped HCR set** — gives a non-ICP refinement that doesn't depend on a good initial pose.

Want me to add either (or both) as a follow-up pass to S10?

message.content: one thing I noticed is that TEASER++ does not assume nonrigid deformation. what about hungarian? ICP and RANSAC both deals with nonrigid, correct? What is FPFH? what does M3 mean? how about P4 and M4?

content.text:
Quick clarification first — your premise has it backwards.

**Rigid / similarity** (no non-rigid deformation modeled):
- **Hungarian** — purely a *correspondence* solver. It takes a fixed cost matrix `D[i,j]` and finds the min-cost assignment. It assumes nothing about deformation; whatever pose model you applied *before* building D is what you get. In our pipeline, LP (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) does the geometric work; Hungarian just picks pairs. So calling it "rigid" or "non-rigid" is a category error — it has no deformation model at all.
- **TEASER++** — rigid (or similarity, in the GNC-TLS variant). Robust to outliers, but the transform class is rigid.
- **ICP** — *standard ICP is rigid.* Non-rigid variants exist (NRICP, CPD-non-rigid) but `M3-ICP` in v2 is rigid/similarity, not non-rigid.
- **RANSAC** — RANSAC is a *meta-algorithm* (random sample → fit hypothesis → score inliers). The deformation model is whatever hypothesis class you give it. With FPFH features the standard pipeline fits a *rigid* transform. RANSAC scales poorly with parameter count, so non-rigid RANSAC is rare.

**Non-rigid** in our plan:
- **TPS** (thin-plate spline) — §4.5 "TPS expansion seeded from anchor pairs"
- CPD-non-rigid, FFD — not in our menu

So none of TEASER / ICP / RANSAC handle non-rigid deformation in their standard forms.

---

**Method codes** (v1 candidate menu, `data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_01/bench/candidate_impls/`):

- **FPFH** = *Fast Point Feature Histograms* (Rusu 2009). A local 3D shape descriptor: for each point, compute a histogram of pairwise angle features (using surface normals) over its neighborhood. Used for *feature-based correspondence proposal* — typical pipeline is FPFH → nearest-neighbor matches → RANSAC to fit a rigid transform. Cell centroids don't have surface normals natively, so FPFH on cell clouds usually means estimating normals from local centroid neighborhoods first.

- **M3-ICP** — `M` = matching/registration family, `M3` = the third such candidate. In our pipeline `M3-ICP-locked` = anisotropic ICP that pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) from LP and refines residual translation/rotation. It's the v2-S03 winner that contributes to the 2.95 gated ensemble.

- **P4** = *Spectral Graph Matching* (Leordeanu & Hebert 2005). Build a graph over each cloud (nodes = cells, edges = spatial neighbors with edge weights). Construct a `(NcNh) × (NcNh)` affinity matrix scoring how compatible each potential pair `(i↔j)` is with each other potential pair `(k↔l)` based on edge similarity. The leading eigenvector gives a soft assignment; discretize to get pairs. Captures *relational* structure (neighbor-of-neighbor consistency) that Hungarian's pairwise distance can't.

- **M4** — per-cell IoU on `cz_voronoi_labels`. Compute the Voronoi tessellation of each centroid set; match cells by IoU of their Voronoi regions after pose alignment, instead of centroid distance. It's a *region-overlap* matcher — handles density heterogeneity better than nearest-centroid because two close-together cells get small Voronoi cells and won't both grab the same HCR target.

The `P` vs `M` prefix in v1 was a loose grouping (P = point/pose-class candidates, M = matching/correspondence-class), not a strict taxonomy — happy to read `_p14_hungarian.py` etc. if you want the exact definitions verified.

message.content: Will it be a good idea to check if FPFH is feasible in our case by looking at the ground-truth data? For example, we know what are matches and what are close but not matches, and look at the features of the matches and near-misses and if the features work well for correspondence.

content.text:
Yes — this is exactly the right move, and it's cheap. A descriptor-discrimination test on GT pairs vs near-misses tells you whether FPFH+RANSAC has any chance *before* you build the pipeline.

**Proposed diagnostic** (no RANSAC, no pose fitting — just a feature-quality check):

1. **Setup per subject**: LP warm-start (locked frame, sz pinned to iter-7) so CZ and HCR live in the same µm zyx frame. Apply the bbox crop + τ=0.3 quality filter so the HCR neighbourhood matches what FPFH+RANSAC would actually see.
2. **Compute FPFH** on CZ centroids and on the cropped HCR centroid set, using the same radius `r_normal` and `r_feature` (e.g., r_normal = 25 µm, r_feature = 50 µm). Normals from local-neighbour PCA, oriented away from pia for consistency.
3. **Define cohorts** for each GT pair `(cz_i ↔ hcr_j)`:
   - **Match** distance: `‖FPFH(cz_i) − FPFH(hcr_j)‖`
   - **Near-miss** distances: `‖FPFH(cz_i) − FPFH(hcr_k)‖` for all `k ≠ j` with `‖hcr_k − hcr_j‖ < R` (e.g., R = 30, 50, 100 µm). The "hard" near-miss set is the most informative.
4. **Metrics, per subject**:
   - **AUC** for match-vs-near-miss separation
   - **Rank of true partner** when sorting all cropped HCR cells by FPFH distance to `cz_i` — report `recall@1, @5, @20`
   - **Baseline comparison**: same metrics but with centroid distance `‖cz_i − hcr_k‖` after LP. If FPFH AUC ≤ centroid-distance AUC, FPFH adds nothing.

**Concerns to flag up-front**:

- FPFH was designed for *dense surface point clouds with reliable normals*. Cell centroids are sparse and "normals" are PCA-of-neighbours — meaningful only where the local distribution has a clear flat axis (near the pia surface yes; deep cortex maybe not).
- CZ↔HCR density mismatch matters: HCR has more cells in the bbox than CZ, so even with correct pose the neighbourhood "looks" different. The crop + quality filter narrows this, but doesn't fix it.
- This will inherit the same LP-residual ceiling on 755252/782149 — but FPFH is *invariant to local rotation/translation*, so a uniform LP error shouldn't hurt it. **Local non-uniform** distortion is what kills FPFH, and that's exactly what we'd learn from the diagnostic.

**Worth adding**: a simpler **PPF (Point Pair Features)** or **local-density / k-NN-distance histogram** baseline — these are also rotation-invariant, simpler, and arguably better suited to sparse centroid clouds. If a 5-line k-NN-distance descriptor matches FPFH, FPFH isn't earning its complexity.

**Output**: one table per subject — AUC and recall@K for FPFH, PPF, k-NN-dist, centroid-distance baseline. ~1–2 hours of work using Open3D.

**Stop rule**: if best-feature AUC < ~0.80 on ≥4 subjects, FPFH+RANSAC won't beat the v2 ensemble; skip P15 and move on. If AUC ≥ 0.85 on most subjects, it's worth the full pipeline run.

Want me to set this up as a session (e.g., `v3_S12_fpfh_feasibility/`)?

lastPrompt: Will it be a good idea to check if FPFH is feasible in our case by looking at the ground-truth data? For example, we know what are matches and what are close but not matches, and look at the features…

message.content: First of all, there is no need to use LP warm-start. You can simply use coregistered pairs for TPS transformation, and use the overlapping bbox from the ground-truth registration. Can normal be the surface of HCR? Also not all HCR ROIs - it should be GFP+ ROIs (looser threshold, not the strict threshold (tell me, where did I use the stricter threshold?)) with quailty threshold 0.5 (from binary good vs bad prediction). TEst FPFH, PPF, and k-NN-dist, centroid-distance baseline.

content.text:
Good redesign — using GT-TPS for alignment cleanly isolates "is the descriptor doing work?" from "did the pose converge?", and pia-normals fix the weakest link in FPFH-on-centroids.

**Where the stricter threshold was used:**

- **Session 07b** (strict-GFP+ scale, FAILED): per-channel **GMM-intersection cutoff** — the threshold = the value where the two-Gaussian likelihoods cross. This was the explicitly-strict variant.
- **Session 07c** (BIC-GFP+, FAILED): BIC-selected **K ∈ [2, 6]** GMM with GT-Procrustes-mapped density gating. Less strict than 07b but still aggressive.

The **looser** threshold you're pointing me to is **subgoal 04/R1 v2.2 defaults** (`feedback_threshold_generalisability` + `project_subgoal_01_02_gfp_thresholds`):
- spot: `peakgauss3_density_p0.1`
- intensity: `peakgauss3_mean_bg_p1`

Distribution-driven (per-subject percentile cuts), not GMM-intersection. Confirm this is the looser threshold you mean before I wire it in.

**Revised diagnostic design (v3_S12_fpfh_feasibility):**

1. **Alignment**: GT-TPS — fit `scipy.interpolate.Rbf(thin_plate)` from GT (cz_centroid → hcr_centroid) pairs per subject, warp full CZ centroid set into HCR µm frame. No LP, no Stage A/B.
2. **Bbox**: from GT-TPS-warped CZ extents (no expansion needed since pose is GT-clean; or +20 µm pad).
3. **HCR cell set**: `(GFP+ via v2.2 looser threshold)  ∧  (roi_quality_binary_v5d ≥ 0.5)`. Tag GT cells separately so we can measure GT survival under this filter.
4. **Normals from HCR surface**: for each cell (CZ or HCR), project onto the HCR pia surface (the one `surface_registration_v2` already produces) and take the surface normal at the projection point. Both clouds share the same normal field → FPFH is comparable across modalities.
5. **Descriptors tested**:
   - **FPFH** (Open3D) with pia-projected normals, `r_feature` swept over {30, 50, 80} µm
   - **PPF** (Point-Pair Features, Drost 2010): for each cell, histogram of `(d_ij, ∠(n_i, d_ij), ∠(n_j, d_ij), ∠(n_i, n_j))` over neighbours within R µm
   - **k-NN distance histogram**: 1D histogram of distances to k-th NN for k ∈ {1..20}
   - **Centroid-distance baseline**: `‖cz_i − hcr_j‖` after GT-TPS (this should already be near-zero on GT pairs — it's the floor)
6. **Match vs near-miss**:
   - Match: GT pair `(cz_i ↔ hcr_j)`
   - Near-miss: HCR cells within R = 30 / 50 / 100 µm of `hcr_j`, excluding `j`
7. **Metrics, per subject × descriptor × R**:
   - AUC
   - recall@1/5/20 of true HCR partner when ranking the cropped HCR set by descriptor distance
8. **Stop rule**: descriptor only earns FPFH+RANSAC promotion if AUC ≥ 0.85 on ≥4/6 subjects AND beats centroid-distance under realistic (LP-not-GT) pose noise.

**One open question before I run:** for centroid-distance baseline — do you want it computed under GT-TPS (in which case it's trivially perfect on GT pairs and only the near-miss spread tells us anything), or also under LP warm-start so we have a "what FPFH must beat" reference? I'd suggest both: GT-TPS shows descriptor *ceiling*, LP shows what it must clear in practice.

Confirm the looser-threshold pointer and the centroid-baseline-under-LP add, and I'll set up `v3_S12_fpfh_feasibility/`.

content: Goal: test if FPFH/PPF/k-NN-dist descriptors can beat centroid distance for CZ↔HCR cell matching (Grand Plan v3 §4.1 follow-up). Next action: confirm the looser GFP+ threshold is v2.2 defaults (`density_p0.1` + `mean_bg_p1`) and whether to add LP-pose centroid baseline, then set up `v3_S12_fpfh_feasibility/`. (disable recaps in /config)

input.prompt:
I'm setting up an FPFH descriptor feasibility test (session v3_S12_fpfh_feasibility) for cell-cell matching. Before writing the runner, I need to locate several APIs in this codebase. Please report concise pointers (file path + function/symbol name + 1-line description) for each:

1. **Strict GFP+ definition (session 07b)** — per-channel GMM-intersection cutoff that selects "strict GFP+" HCR ROIs. Search under `/root/capsule/data/claude_data/sessions/` and `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/`. Find the function/script that, given a subject, returns a per-cell boolean mask of strict-GFP+ HCR ROIs, AND tell me exactly what column or expression to use. If it's been re-exported into `dev_code` give that path; otherwise give the original session path.

2. **GT cell-cell pair data** — the ground-truth `(cz_id, hcr_id)` pairs for the 6 benchmark subjects. The S10 driver at `code/sessions/v3_S10_revival_pass/run_constrained_revival.py` already loads them — open it and report the exact loader function name, module path, and the format it returns (column names, frame). Also give the list of 6 benchmark subject IDs.

3. **Locked-prior warm-start (LP)** — `apply_to_cz_um(LP, cz_um)` style API used in S10. Report the module that provides `LocalPriorWarm` / `locked_prior_warm` (or whatever it's called), the function that returns the warmed CZ centroids in HCR µm zyx, and how `sz` is pinned to iter-7. The S10 driver shows the usage; just confirm the import paths.

4. **HCR pia surface** — for a subject, get the HCR pia (outer brain) surface so I can project centroids onto it and read the surface normal at the projection. Look in `code/dev_code/surfaces_iter08.py` and `code/dev_code/surface_registration_v2.py`. Report:
   - the function that returns the HCR top/pia surface (parameterised as a height map or an implicit surface)
   - whether normals are returned or need to be derived from gradients
   - what frame/units the surface is in (HCR µm zyx? voxel?)

5. **ROI quality stage-2 v5d binary score** — file path of the parquet/csv that holds `roi_quality_stage2_binary_score_v5d` per (subject, hcr_id), AND the loader pattern (the S10 driver uses it with τ=0.3; I need τ=0.5). Quote the exact column names.

6. **Open3D and pyteaser availability** — is `open3d` importable in this env? Check `pip show open3d` or `python -c "import open3d"`. We need it for FPFH.

Also report:
- any `analyze_subject` or `Subject` helper that bundles these per-subject — the S10 driver's `run_one_subject(sid)` likely shows the canonical pattern; quote its imports and the first ~30 lines.

Keep response concise — just paths, symbols, and 1-line descriptions per item. Mark anything you couldn't find.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the pieces. Let me compile the final report:

## Summary Report: FPFH Descriptor Feasibility Test — API Pointers

### 1. **Strict GFP+ definition (session 07b)**

**File:** `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/07b_gfp_intersection_threshold.py`

**Main functions:**
- `analyze_subject(sid: str) -> GmmIntersection` (line 340) — per-subject GMM-intersection analysis
- `strict_gfp_df(sid: str, cutoff_linear: float) -> pd.DataFrame` (line 453) — returns filtered HCR GFP+ table by linear cutoff

**Column to use:** 
- **For spot subjects** (788406, 790322, 767018, 782149): filter on `"density"` column
- **For intensity-only** (755252, 767022): filter on `"mean_minus_bg"` column

**Mask:** Use `df[df["density"] >= cutoff]` or `df[df["mean_minus_bg"] >= cutoff]` on the DataFrame returned by `strict_gfp_df()`.

---

### 2. **GT cell-cell pair data**

**File:** `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/benchmark_data_loader.py` (lines 715, 556–564)

**Loader function:** `load_subject(subject_id: str) -> SubjectData`

**GT pairs access:** `s.coreg_table` — a DataFrame loaded from `*coreg_table.csv` in the subject's coreg dir

**Columns:** `['cz_id', 'hcr_id']` (integers)

edges = 10 ** np.linspace(log_x.min() - 0.1, log_x.max() + 0.1, 100)
    else:
        edges = np.exp(np.linspace(log_x.min() - 0.1, log_x.max() + 0.1, 100))
    ax.hist(pos, bins=edges, color="#94a3b8", alpha=0.6, label="all cells")
    ax.axvline(cutoff_linear, color="#cc3333", lw=2.0,
               label=f"strict cutoff: {cutoff_linear:.3g}")
    if np.isfinite(v22_cutoff_linear) and v22_cutoff_linear > 0:
        ax.axvline(v22_cutoff_linear, color="#3b7dd8", lw=1.2, ls="--",
                   label=f"v2.2 cutoff: {v22_cutoff_linear:.3g}")
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel(f"{feature_name} (linear, log-axis)")
    ax.set_ylabel("count")
    ax.set_title(f"{sid} — linear-axis view")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, which="both", alpha=0.3)

    # BIC sweep panel
    ax = axes[2]
    if sweep_entries:
        ks = [e["n_components"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        bics = [e["bic"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        aics = [e["aic"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        ax.plot(ks, bics, "o-", color="#268bd2", label="BIC")
        ax.plot(ks, aics, "s--", color="#859900", label="AIC", alpha=0.6)
        best_k = fit["n_components"]
        best_bic = fit["bic"]
        ax.axvline(best_k, color="#cc3333", lw=1.5, alpha=0.6,
                   label=f"best K = {best_k}")
        ax.scatter([best_k], [best_bic], color="#cc3333", zorder=5, s=80)
    ax.set_xlabel("n_components")
    ax.set_ylabel("information criterion")
    ax.set_title(f"{sid} — BIC/AIC sweep K=2..6")
    ax.legend(loc="best", fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=140)
    plt.close(fig)


def analyze_subject(sid: str) -> GmmIntersection:
    s = load_subject(sid)
    coreg = s.coreg_table
    coreg_hcr_ids = set(int(x) for x in coreg["hcr_id"].values)

    # v2.2 cutoff (linear): back-derived from the loaded gfp_df.
    # For spot subjects, v2.2 thresholds by density >= cutoff;
    # for intensity, by mean_minus_bg >= cutoff. Use the min of the
    # kept rows' relevant feature as the effective cutoff.
    v22_df = s.hcr_gfp_df
    n_v22 = int(len(v22_df))

    if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
        df_feat = _load_spot_feature(sid)
        feat_values = df_feat["density"].values.astype(float)
        log_x = np.log(feat_values[feat_values > 0])
        sweep = fit_gmm_sweep(log_x, k_min=2, k_max=6)
        fit = sweep["best"]
        cutoff = float(np.exp(fit["intersection_log"]))
        if "density" in v22_df.columns and n_v22:
            v22_cut = float(v22_df["density"].min())
        else:
            v22_cut = float("nan")
        feature_name = "density"
        log_base = "ln"
    elif sid in INTENSITY_SUBJECTS:
        df_feat = _load_intensity_feature(sid)
        feat_values = df_feat["mean_minus_bg"].values.astype(float)
        log_x = np.log10(feat_values[feat_values > 0])
        sweep = fit_gmm_sweep(log_x, k_min=2, k_max=6)
        fit = sweep["best"]
        cutoff = float(10.0 ** fit["intersection_log"])
        if "mean_minus_bg" in v22_df.columns and n_v22:
            v22_cut = float(v22_df["mean_minus_bg"].min())
        elif {"mean", "background"}.issubset(v22_df.columns) and n_v22:
            v22_cut = float((v22_df["mean"] - v22_df["background"]).min())
        else:
            v22_cut = float("nan")
        feature_name = "mean_minus_bg"
        log_base = "log10"
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"unknown subject class for {sid}")

    # Apply strict cutoff and compute coverage
    mask = df_feat[feature_name].values.astype(float) >= cutoff
    df_strict = df_feat[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    n_strict = int(len(df_strict))
    strict_ids = set(int(x) for x in df_strict["hcr_id"].values)
    coreg_kept = len(coreg_hcr_ids & strict_ids)
    coreg_cov = coreg_kept / max(len(coreg_hcr_ids), 1)

    # Sanity
    notes = []
    mu_right = fit["means"][-1]
    mu_next = fit["means"][-2]
    interior_ok = (mu_next < fit["intersection_log"] < mu_right)
    if not interior_ok or fit["no_interior_root"]:
        notes.append("intersection outside top-two mean bracket")
    if n_strict < 300:
        notes.append(f"n_strict={n_strict} < 300")
    if coreg_cov < 0.80:
        notes.append(f"coreg_coverage_strict={coreg_cov:.3f} < 0.80")
    sanity = (len(notes) == 0)

    # Plot
    out_png = FIG_DIR / f"gmm_threshold_{sid}.png"
    _plot_subject(
        sid, feat_values, fit, cutoff, v22_cut, feature_name, log_base, out_png,
        sweep_entries=sweep["sweep"],
    )

    # Compact sweep entries for JSON storage
    sweep_entries = []
    for entry in sweep["sweep"]:
        if "error" in entry:
            sweep_entries.append({"n_components": entry["n_components"], "error": entry["error"]})
        else:
            sweep_entries.append({
                "n_components": entry["n_components"],
                "bic": entry["bic"],
                "aic": entry["aic"],
                "intersection_log": entry["intersection_log"],
                "no_interior_root": entry["no_interior_root"],
                "means": entry["means"],
            })

    return GmmIntersection(
        subject=sid,
        feature_name=feature_name,
        log_base=log_base,
        n_components=int(fit["n_components"]),
        means_log=fit["means"],
        sigmas_log=fit["sigmas"],
        weights=fit["weights"],
        intersection_log=float(fit["intersection_log"]),
        cutoff_linear=float(cutoff),
        no_interior_root=bool(fit["no_interior_root"]),
        sigma_right_over_next=float(fit["sigma_right"] / max(fit["sigma_next"], 1e-12)),
        bic=float(fit["bic"]),
        bic_sweep=sweep_entries,
        n_total=int(len(df_feat)),
        n_positive=int((feat_values > 0).sum()),
        n_strict=n_strict,
        n_v22_reference=n_v22,
        cutoff_v22_linear=float(v22_cut) if np.isfinite(v22_cut) else float("nan"),
        coreg_total=int(len(coreg_hcr_ids)),
        coreg_kept_strict=int(coreg_kept),
        coreg_coverage_strict=float(coreg_cov),
        sanity_passed=sanity,
        sanity_notes=notes,
    )


def strict_gfp_df(sid: str, cutoff_linear: float) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return the strict GFP+ table for a subject (for driver's monkey-patch)."""
    if sid in SPOT_SUBJECTS:
        df_feat = _load_spot_feature(sid)
        return df_feat[df_feat["density"].values.astype(float) >= cutoff_linear].reset_index(drop=True)
    if sid in INTENSITY_SUBJECTS:
        df_feat = _load_intensity_feature(sid)
        return df_feat[df_feat["mean_minus_bg"].values.astype(float) >= cutoff_linear].reset_index(drop=True)
    raise ValueError(f"unknown subject class for {sid}")


def main():
    results = []
    for sid in BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS:
        try:
            r = analyze_subject(sid)
            results.append(asdict(r))
            print(
                f"  {sid}  cutoff={r.cutoff_linear:.4g}  n_strict={r.n_strict}  "
                f"coreg_cov={r.coreg_coverage_strict:.3f}  "
                f"sanity={'OK' if r.sanity_passed else '/'.join(r.sanity_notes)}"
            )
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  {sid} FAILED: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
            results.append({"subject": sid, "status": f"error: {e}"})
    out = SESSION_DIR / "gmm_threshold_results.json"
    with open(out, "w") as f:
        json.dump(results, f, indent=2, default=lambda v: v.tolist()
                  if hasattr(v, "tolist") else str(v))
    print(f"Wrote {out}")
    print(f"Figures: {FIG_DIR}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

1	"""Scale-only GFP+ threshold via GMM-intersection.
2	
3	Session 07 Part D showed the project-wide v2.2 GFP+ set (rightmost
4	GMM-3 component lower tail, `peakgauss3_density_p0.1` / `...p1`) has
5	subject- and depth-dependent detection bias that contaminates any
6	centroid-based scale estimator. This module builds a *stricter*
7	scale-only GFP+ threshold at the Bayes-optimal intersection between
8	the top-two GMM components on log(feature), deflating the false
9	positives.
10	
11	**Important:** this threshold is **not** used by the project-wide
12	loader. It is computed here fresh for each subject and applied as an
13	in-memory replacement of ``SubjectData.hcr_gfp_df`` inside the
14	session 07b driver.
15	
16	Feature choice:
17	* spot subjects (788406, 790322, 767018, 782149):
18	  GMM-4 on ``log(density > 0)`` where ``density = counts / volume``.
19	* intensity-only (755252, 767022):
20	  GMM-2 on ``log10(mean − bg > 0)`` from
21	  ``cell_data_mean_{sid}_R1.csv`` channel 488.
22	
23	The rightmost and second-rightmost GMM components are selected by
24	sorting on ``μ`` ascending and taking indices K−1 and K−2. Their
25	Bayes-optimal intersection is the stricter cutoff.
26	
27	Sanity gate per subject (blocks scale estimation if any violated):
28	1. the intersection falls strictly inside [min μ, max μ] of the pair
29	   (i.e. not an extrapolated root);
30	2. ``n_strict >= 300`` surviving HCR cells;
31	3. ``coreg_coverage_strict >= 0.80`` — the strict cutoff may drop
32	   some coreg-matched HCR cells but not most of them; if more are
33	   dropped than 20 %, the GMM likely split inside the signal peak.
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import json
38	import sys
39	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
40	from pathlib import Path
41	
42	import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
43	import numpy as np
44	import pandas as pd
45	from sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture
46	
47	_DEV = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
48	if str(_DEV) not in sys.path:
49	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_DEV))
50	
51	from benchmark_data_loader import (
52	    DATA_DIR,
53	    BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS,
54	    _aggregate_spots_from_hcr,
55	    load_subject,
56	)
57	
58	SESSION_DIR = Path("/root/capsule/code/sessions/07b_scale_clean_gfp")
59	FIG_DIR = SESSION_DIR / "figures"
60	FIG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
61	
62	SPOT_SUBJECTS = {"788406", "790322", "767018", "782149"}
63	INTENSITY_SUBJECTS = {"755252", "767022"}
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class GmmIntersection:
68	    subject: str
69	    feature_name: str          # 'density' | 'mean_minus_bg'
70	    log_base: str              # 'ln' | 'log10'
71	    n_components: int          # BIC-selected K
72	    means_log: list            # ascending by mean
73	    sigmas_log: list
74	    weights: list
75	    intersection_log: float    # in log-domain
76	    cutoff_linear: float       # linear-domain cutoff
77	    no_interior_root: bool
78	    sigma_right_over_next: float
79	    bic: float                 # BIC of the selected K
80	    bic_sweep: list            # [{n_components, bic, aic, intersection_log, ...}] over K=2..6
81	    n_total: int               # rows in the raw feature table before >0 filter
82	    n_positive: int            # rows with feature > 0 (used for GMM)
83	    n_strict: int              # rows passing the strict cutoff
84	    n_v22_reference: int       # rows that the v2.2 loader keeps (from SubjectData)
85	    cutoff_v22_linear: float   # v2.2 threshold in the same linear feature
86	    coreg_total: int
87	    coreg_kept_strict: int
88	    coreg_coverage_strict: float
89	    sanity_passed: bool
90	    sanity_notes: list
91	
92	
93	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
94	# GMM intersection math
95	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
96	def gaussian_intersection(
97	    mu1: float, s1: float, w1: float,
98	    mu2: float, s2: float, w2: float,
99	) -> tuple[float, bool]:
100	    """Solve w1·N(x; μ1, σ1) == w2·N(x; μ2, σ2) for x; return (x, no_interior_root).
101	
102	    Preconditions: mu1 < mu2 (caller ensures ascending order). We select the
103	    root in (mu1, mu2). Returns midpoint and sets ``no_interior_root=True`` if
104	    no interior root exists (extreme weights/variances).
105	    """
106	    if mu1 == mu2:
107	        return float(mu1), True
108	
109	    v1 = s1 * s1
110	    v2 = s2 * s2
111	
112	    # Degenerate equal-variance → linear equation
113	    if abs(v1 - v2) < 1e-12:
114	        denom = mu2 - mu1
115	        x = 0.5 * (mu1 + mu2) + (v1 / denom) * np.log(w2 / max(w1, 1e-300))
116	        inside = (mu1 < x) and (x < mu2)
117	        return float(x), (not inside)
118	
119	    A = 1.0 / (2.0 * v2) - 1.0 / (2.0 * v1)
120	    B = mu1 / v1 - mu2 / v2

file.content:
"""Scale-only GFP+ threshold via GMM-intersection.

Session 07 Part D showed the project-wide v2.2 GFP+ set (rightmost
GMM-3 component lower tail, `peakgauss3_density_p0.1` / `...p1`) has
subject- and depth-dependent detection bias that contaminates any
centroid-based scale estimator. This module builds a *stricter*
scale-only GFP+ threshold at the Bayes-optimal intersection between
the top-two GMM components on log(feature), deflating the false
positives.

**Important:** this threshold is **not** used by the project-wide
loader. It is computed here fresh for each subject and applied as an
in-memory replacement of ``SubjectData.hcr_gfp_df`` inside the
session 07b driver.

Feature choice:
* spot subjects (788406, 790322, 767018, 782149):
  GMM-4 on ``log(density > 0)`` where ``density = counts / volume``.
* intensity-only (755252, 767022):
  GMM-2 on ``log10(mean − bg > 0)`` from
  ``cell_data_mean_{sid}_R1.csv`` channel 488.

The rightmost and second-rightmost GMM components are selected by
sorting on ``μ`` ascending and taking indices K−1 and K−2. Their
Bayes-optimal intersection is the stricter cutoff.

Sanity gate per subject (blocks scale estimation if any violated):
1. the intersection falls strictly inside [min μ, max μ] of the pair
   (i.e. not an extrapolated root);
2. ``n_strict >= 300`` surviving HCR cells;
3. ``coreg_coverage_strict >= 0.80`` — the strict cutoff may drop
   some coreg-matched HCR cells but not most of them; if more are
   dropped than 20 %, the GMM likely split inside the signal peak.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sys
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture

_DEV = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
if str(_DEV) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_DEV))

from benchmark_data_loader import (
    DATA_DIR,
    BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS,
    _aggregate_spots_from_hcr,
    load_subject,
)

SESSION_DIR = Path("/root/capsule/code/sessions/07b_scale_clean_gfp")
FIG_DIR = SESSION_DIR / "figures"
FIG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

SPOT_SUBJECTS = {"788406", "790322", "767018", "782149"}
INTENSITY_SUBJECTS = {"755252", "767022"}


@dataclass
class GmmIntersection:
    subject: str
    feature_name: str          # 'density' | 'mean_minus_bg'
    log_base: str              # 'ln' | 'log10'
    n_components: int          # BIC-selected K
    means_log: list            # ascending by mean
    sigmas_log: list
    weights: list
    intersection_log: float    # in log-domain
    cutoff_linear: float       # linear-domain cutoff
    no_interior_root: bool
    sigma_right_over_next: float
    bic: float                 # BIC of the selected K
    bic_sweep: list            # [{n_components, bic, aic, intersection_log, ...}] over K=2..6
    n_total: int               # rows in the raw feature table before >0 filter
    n_positive: int            # rows with feature > 0 (used for GMM)
    n_strict: int              # rows passing the strict cutoff
    n_v22_reference: int       # rows that the v2.2 loader keeps (from SubjectData)
    cutoff_v22_linear: float   # v2.2 threshold in the same linear feature
    coreg_total: int
    coreg_kept_strict: int
    coreg_coverage_strict: float
    sanity_passed: bool
    sanity_notes: list


# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# GMM intersection math
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
def gaussian_intersection(
    mu1: float, s1: float, w1: float,
    mu2: float, s2: float, w2: float,
) -> tuple[float, bool]:
    """Solve w1·N(x; μ1, σ1) == w2·N(x; μ2, σ2) for x; return (x, no_interior_root).

    Preconditions: mu1 < mu2 (caller ensures ascending order). We select the
    root in (mu1, mu2). Returns midpoint and sets ``no_interior_root=True`` if
    no interior root exists (extreme weights/variances).
    """
    if mu1 == mu2:
        return float(mu1), True

    v1 = s1 * s1
    v2 = s2 * s2

    # Degenerate equal-variance → linear equation
    if abs(v1 - v2) < 1e-12:
        denom = mu2 - mu1
        x = 0.5 * (mu1 + mu2) + (v1 / denom) * np.log(w2 / max(w1, 1e-300))
        inside = (mu1 < x) and (x < mu2)
        return float(x), (not inside)

    A = 1.0 / (2.0 * v2) - 1.0 / (2.0 * v1)
    B = mu1 / v1 - mu2 / v2

B = mu1 / v1 - mu2 / v2
    C = (
        np.log(max(w1, 1e-300)) - np.log(max(w2, 1e-300))
        + 0.5 * (np.log(v2) - np.log(v1))
        + (mu2 * mu2) / (2.0 * v2)
        - (mu1 * mu1) / (2.0 * v1)
    )
    disc = B * B - 4.0 * A * C
    if disc < 0:
        return 0.5 * (mu1 + mu2), True
    sq = float(np.sqrt(disc))
    r1 = (-B + sq) / (2.0 * A)
    r2 = (-B - sq) / (2.0 * A)
    candidates = [r for r in (r1, r2) if mu1 < r < mu2]
    if not candidates:
        return 0.5 * (mu1 + mu2), True
    # Prefer the root closer to the weighted midpoint (for robustness)
    ref = 0.5 * (mu1 + mu2)
    best = min(candidates, key=lambda r: abs(r - ref))
    return float(best), False


def fit_gmm_intersection(
    log_values: np.ndarray, n_components: int, random_state: int = 0
) -> dict:
    """Fit a K-component GMM on log-values and return intersection between
    the rightmost (K−1) and second-rightmost (K−2) components."""
    X = np.asarray(log_values, dtype=float).reshape(-1, 1)
    gmm = GaussianMixture(
        n_components=n_components, n_init=5, random_state=random_state
    ).fit(X)
    order = np.argsort(gmm.means_.ravel())
    mus = gmm.means_.ravel()[order]
    sigmas = np.sqrt(gmm.covariances_.ravel()[order])
    weights = gmm.weights_[order]
    # Rightmost (index K-1) and second-rightmost (K-2)
    mu1, s1, w1 = mus[-2], sigmas[-2], weights[-2]
    mu2, s2, w2 = mus[-1], sigmas[-1], weights[-1]
    x_int, no_interior = gaussian_intersection(mu1, s1, w1, mu2, s2, w2)
    return {
        "means": mus.tolist(),
        "sigmas": sigmas.tolist(),
        "weights": weights.tolist(),
        "intersection_log": float(x_int),
        "no_interior_root": bool(no_interior),
        "sigma_right": float(sigmas[-1]),
        "sigma_next": float(sigmas[-2]),
        "bic": float(gmm.bic(X)),
        "aic": float(gmm.aic(X)),
        "n_components": int(n_components),
    }


def fit_gmm_sweep(
    log_values: np.ndarray,
    k_min: int = 2,
    k_max: int = 6,
    random_state: int = 0,
) -> dict:
    """Sweep ``n_components`` over ``[k_min, k_max]`` and pick the best K by BIC.

    Returns a dict with:
      * ``best``: the fit for the BIC-winning K (same shape as
        ``fit_gmm_intersection``'s return value).
      * ``sweep``: list of per-K dicts with BIC, AIC, means, intersection_log,
        so the driver can log a full sweep table.
    The selection rule is plain argmin of BIC — no within-1-SE heuristic,
    because we want the strictest density fit the data supports.
    """
    X = np.asarray(log_values, dtype=float)
    sweep = []
    for k in range(k_min, k_max + 1):
        try:
            fit = fit_gmm_intersection(X, n_components=k, random_state=random_state)
            sweep.append(fit)
        except Exception as e:
            sweep.append({"n_components": k, "error": f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}"})
    valid = [f for f in sweep if "bic" in f]
    if not valid:
        raise RuntimeError("GMM sweep produced no valid fits")
    best = min(valid, key=lambda f: f["bic"])
    return {"best": best, "sweep": sweep}


# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Feature loaders (no loader modification)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
def _load_spot_feature(sid: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return DataFrame with columns [hcr_id, density] for a spot subject.

    Source priority matches ``benchmark_data_loader._load_gfp``:
      1. ``*spot_488_counts.csv`` in the subject's coreg dir.
      2. ``_aggregate_spots_from_hcr`` fallback.
    """
    s = load_subject(sid)
    # Use SubjectData paths: s.coreg_dir / s.hcr_dir
    coreg_dir = s.coreg_dir
    hcr_dir = s.hcr_dir
    spot_files = list(Path(coreg_dir).glob("*spot_488_counts.csv"))
    if spot_files:
        df = pd.read_csv(spot_files[0])
        if "density" not in df.columns and {"counts", "volume"}.issubset(df.columns):
            df["density"] = df["counts"] / df["volume"]
        return df[["hcr_id", "density"]].dropna()
    agg = _aggregate_spots_from_hcr(Path(hcr_dir))
    if agg is None or len(agg) == 0:
        raise RuntimeError(f"No spot feature available for {sid}")
    if "density" not in agg.columns:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Spot data for {sid} has no density column")
    return agg[["hcr_id", "density"]].dropna()


def _load_intensity_feature(sid: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return DataFrame with columns [hcr_id, mean_minus_bg] for intensity subject."""
    p = Path(DATA_DIR) / f"cell_data_mean_{sid}_R1.csv"
    if not p.exists():
        raise RuntimeError(f"No intensity CSV for {sid}: {p}")
    df = pd.read_csv(p)
    if "channel" in df.columns:
        df = df[df["channel"] == 488]
    rename_candidates = {"cell_id": "hcr_id", "id": "hcr_id"}
    for k, v in rename_candidates.items():
        if k in df.columns:
            df = df.rename(columns={k: v})
    df = df.copy()
    df["mean_minus_bg"] = df["mean"].astype(float) - df["background"].astype(float)
    return df[["hcr_id", "mean_minus_bg"]].dropna()


# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Per-subject analyse + plot
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
def _plot_subject(
    sid: str, feat_values: np.ndarray, fit: dict, cutoff_linear: float,
    v22_cutoff_linear: float, feature_name: str, log_base: str,
    out_path: Path, sweep_entries: list | None = None,
):
    is_log10 = (log_base == "log10")
    logfn = np.log10 if is_log10 else np.log
    expfn = (lambda x: 10.0 ** x) if is_log10 else np.exp
    base_str = "log10" if is_log10 else "ln"

    pos = feat_values[feat_values > 0]
    log_x = logfn(pos)
    mus = fit["means"]
    sigmas = fit["sigmas"]
    weights = fit["weights"]
    x_int = fit["intersection_log"]

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 4.4))
    # linear density panel (log-x)
    ax = axes[0]
    bins = np.linspace(log_x.min() - 0.2, log_x.max() + 0.2, 100)
    ax.hist(log_x, bins=bins, density=True, alpha=0.55, color="#94a3b8",
            label=f"{base_str}({feature_name})")
    xs = np.linspace(bins[0], bins[-1], 400)
    total = np.zeros_like(xs)
    from scipy.stats import norm
    for mu, sig, w in zip(mus, sigmas, weights):
        comp = w * norm.pdf(xs, mu, sig)
        ax.plot(xs, comp, lw=1.0, alpha=0.8)
        total = total + comp
    ax.plot(xs, total, lw=1.5, color="black", label="GMM total")
    ax.axvline(x_int, color="#cc3333", lw=2.0, label=f"strict cutoff ({base_str}): {x_int:.3f}")
    if np.isfinite(v22_cutoff_linear) and v22_cutoff_linear > 0:
        v22_log = logfn(v22_cutoff_linear)
        ax.axvline(v22_log, color="#3b7dd8", lw=1.2, ls="--",
                   label=f"v2.2 cutoff ({base_str}): {v22_log:.3f}")
    ax.set_xlabel(f"{base_str}({feature_name})")
    ax.set_ylabel("density")
    ax.set_title(f"{sid} — {feature_name} histogram + GMM-{len(mus)} + intersection")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Right panel: linear-axis histogram zoomed around the two top components
    ax = axes[1]
    # show linear-domain feature on log-x axis (matplotlib log-x)
    edges = np.logspace(log_x.min() - 0.1, log_x.max() + 0.1, 100) if not is_log10 \
        else np.logspace((log_x.min() - 0.1), (log_x.max() + 0.1), 100)
    if is_log10:
        edges = 10 ** np.linspace(log_x.min() - 0.1, log_x.max() + 0.1, 100)
    else:
        edges = np.exp(np.linspace(log_x.min() - 0.1, log_x.max() + 0.1, 100))
    ax.hist(pos, bins=edges, color="#94a3b8", alpha=0.6, label="all cells")
    ax.axvline(cutoff_linear, color="#cc3333", lw=2.0,
               label=f"strict cutoff: {cutoff_linear:.3g}")
    if np.isfinite(v22_cutoff_linear) and v22_cutoff_linear > 0:
        ax.axvline(v22_cutoff_linear, color="#3b7dd8", lw=1.2, ls="--",
                   label=f"v2.2 cutoff: {v22_cutoff_linear:.3g}")
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel(f"{feature_name} (linear, log-axis)")
    ax.set_ylabel("count")
    ax.set_title(f"{sid} — linear-axis view")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, which="both", alpha=0.3)

    # BIC sweep panel
    ax = axes[2]
    if sweep_entries:
        ks = [e["n_components"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        bics = [e["bic"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        aics = [e["aic"] for e in sweep_entries if "bic" in e]
        ax.plot(ks, bics, "o-", color="#268bd2", label="BIC")
        ax.plot(ks, aics, "s--", color="#859900", label="AIC", alpha=0.6)
        best_k = fit["n_components"]
        best_bic = fit["bic"]
        ax.axvline(best_k, color="#cc3333", lw=1.5, alpha=0.6,
                   label=f"best K = {best_k}")
        ax.scatter([best_k], [best_bic], color="#cc3333", zorder=5, s=80)
    ax.set_xlabel("n_components")
    ax.set_ylabel("information criterion")
    ax.set_title(f"{sid} — BIC/AIC sweep K=2..6")
    ax.legend(loc="best", fontsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=140)
    plt.close(fig)

file.content:
"""v3-S10 constrained revival of v1 candidates (Grand Plan v3 §4.1).

Operating constraints (per user, 2026-04-30):
  * Locked frame from Stage A + Stage B = LP with sz_init = sz_peak (iter-7).
    Pins (R, sxy, sz, t_x, t_y) and the t_z anchor.
  * HCR centroids restricted to the overlap volume defined by the
    warped-CZ bbox in HCR µm (zyx) plus a small expand_um margin.
  * "Valid" HCR ROIs only — Stage-2 binary classifier predicts
    {good, bad_ok}=1 vs {bad, merged}=0; cells with score >= τ are kept.
    Threshold τ = 0.5 (acc@0.5 = 0.846, mean AUC 0.895 per S11 Step 5).

Candidates run in this session:
  * P14 — Hungarian baseline on Euclidean distance (cheapest sanity floor).
  * P1  — TEASER++ / GNC-TLS at small K with crop-local features.

The matchers are reimplemented as standalone functions that take filtered
(cz_warmed, hcr_kept) point clouds (and HCR feature blocks for P1), so the
v1 candidate machinery (`run_candidate`) is bypassed; that machinery
operates on full HCR and there is no clean hook to swap in a filtered set.

Output: per-subject CSV with n_kept HCR cells, r@5/10/20/30, median error.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment
from scipy.spatial.distance import cdist
from scipy.interpolate import Rbf

_V1 = "/root/capsule/data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_01"
_V2_LIB = "/root/capsule/data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_02/lib"
_DEV = "/root/capsule/code/dev_code"
_ITER = "/root/capsule/data/claude_data/sessions/03c_onset_features/iterations"
_SURF8 = "/root/capsule/data/claude_data/sessions/08_surface_vascular_match"
for _p in (_V2_LIB, _V1, _DEV, _ITER, _SURF8):
    if _p not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, _p)

import bench_harness_patch  # noqa: E402

bench_harness_patch.patch()

from benchmark_data_loader import BENCHMARK_SUBJECTS, load_subject  # noqa: E402
from locked_prior_warm import (  # noqa: E402
    apply_to_cz_um,
    compute_locked_prior_warm_start,
)
from lib.centroid_helpers import centroids_um  # noqa: E402
from lib.cell_features import (  # noqa: E402
    extract_cell_features,
    invariant_feature_mask,
)
from bench.harness import compare_to_gt  # noqa: E402
from benchmark_analysis import fit_anisotropic_similarity  # noqa: E402
from lib.centroid_helpers import apply_aniso_fit  # noqa: E402

# Stage-A + Stage-B sz pin (iter-7 image NCC sweep, 6/6 within ±0.30 of GT)
SZ_TABLE = Path(
    "/root/capsule/data/claude_data/full_automatic_execution_02/sessions/"
    "v2_S02_sz_image_sweep/results_iter7_summary.csv"
)
ROI_QUALITY_DIR = Path("/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality")
OUT_DIR = Path("/root/capsule/code/sessions/v3_S10_revival_pass")
OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

ROI_THR_DEFAULT = 0.5  # binary classifier acc@0.5 = 0.846 (good+bad_ok positive)
EXPAND_UM_DEFAULT = 30.0  # margin around warped-CZ bbox in HCR µm
ACCEPT_RADIUS_DEFAULT = 50.0


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# helpers
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def load_sz_pins() -> dict[str, float]:
    df = pd.read_csv(SZ_TABLE)
    df["subject_id"] = df["subject_id"].astype(str)
    return dict(zip(df["subject_id"], df["sz_peak"].astype(float)))


def load_roi_quality(sid: str) -> pd.Series:
    """Per-HCR-id binary roi-quality score (Stage-2 v5d).

    Index: hcr_id (int).  Value: P({good, bad_ok}) ∈ [0, 1].
    """
    p = ROI_QUALITY_DIR / f"{sid}_stage2_binary_score_v5d.parquet"
    df = pd.read_parquet(p)
    df["hcr_id"] = df["hcr_id"].astype(int)
    return df.set_index("hcr_id")["score"]


def overlap_filter(
    cz_warm_zyx: np.ndarray,
    hcr_zyx: np.ndarray,
    hcr_ids: np.ndarray,
    *,
    expand_um: float,
) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
    """Keep HCR cells inside the (warped-CZ bbox + expand_um) volume."""
    lo = cz_warm_zyx.min(axis=0) - expand_um
    hi = cz_warm_zyx.max(axis=0) + expand_um
    in_bbox = ((hcr_zyx >= lo) & (hcr_zyx <= hi)).all(axis=1)
    return in_bbox, lo, hi


def quality_filter(
    hcr_ids: np.ndarray, roi_q: pd.Series, *, thr: float
) -> np.ndarray:
    """Per-HCR-id mask: True iff stage-2 binary score >= thr.

    Cells missing from `roi_q` (rare; v3_S11 reports ~7-9 % not_found_in_seg)
    are treated as low-quality and rejected.
    """
    scores = roi_q.reindex(hcr_ids.astype(int)).to_numpy(dtype=float)
    scores = np.where(np.isnan(scores), 0.0, scores)
